plan: 1. **Add domain type `ResourceTimingEntry`** in `src/domain/types.ts` — Define an interface capturing same-origin resource timing fields: `name` (URL), `duration`, `transferSize`, `encodedBodySize`, `decodedBodySize`, `startTime`, `responseEnd`, and `timestampMs`. Keep it pure (no browser API types). This gives downstream consumers (US-009 aggregator, snapshot-to-scene mapper) a typed contract.

2. **Add normalization helper `normalizeLoadDuration`** in `src/domain/normalize.ts` — Pure function mapping a duration (ms) to 0–1 range with sensible defaults (e.g., 0–5000ms). Follows existing `normalizeRtt`/`normalizeJitter` pattern. Add `computeMeanDuration` helper to average durations from an array of `ResourceTimingEntry`.

3. **Create `src/metrics/resourceTiming.ts`** — Core implementation file with: (a) `isResourceTimingSupported(): boolean` — feature-detects `PerformanceObserver` and `'resource'` entry type via `PerformanceObserver.supportedEntryTypes`; (b) `extractTimingEntry(entry: PerformanceResourceTiming): ResourceTimingEntry` — pure mapper from browser API type to domain type, filtering to same-origin entries only; (c) `ResourceTimingCollector` class with `start()`, `stop()`, `getEntries(): ResourceTimingEntry[]` methods — uses `PerformanceObserver` to observe `'resource'` entries, maintains a rolling window (default 50), and gracefully no-ops if the API is unavailable.

5. **Write unit tests `tests/unit/metrics/resourceTiming.test.ts`** — Test: (a) `isResourceTimingSupported` returns false when PerformanceObserver is absent and true when present with 'resource' in supportedEntryTypes; (b) `extractTimingEntry` correctly maps fields from a mock PerformanceResourceTiming entry; (c) `ResourceTimingCollector` gracefully no-ops (start/stop don't throw, getEntries returns empty array) when API is unsupported; (d) rolling window eviction works correctly; (e) `computeMeanDuration` and `normalizeLoadDuration` edge cases (empty array, NaN, single entry).

6. **Write unit tests `tests/unit/domain/normalize.test.ts`** — Add tests for `normalizeLoadDuration` and `computeMeanDuration` to the existing normalize test file, covering: zero duration, max duration, values beyond range, NaN/Infinity inputs, empty arrays.

7. **Write E2E smoke test `tests/e2e/resource-timing.spec.ts`** — Playwright test that: (a) navigates to the app; (b) verifies `PerformanceObserver` is available in the browser context; (c) dynamically imports and starts `ResourceTimingCollector`; (d) triggers a fetch to `/probe.txt` and waits briefly; (e) asserts at least one entry is collected with a positive duration; (f) verifies no console errors.

8. **Create ADR `docs/adr/008-resource-timing-collection.md`** — Document decisions: use PerformanceObserver (not `performance.getEntriesByType` polling) for efficiency; same-origin entries only (cross-origin durations may be zero per spec); rolling window of 50 entries; graceful degradation pattern; duration as primary metric with transferSize as secondary when available.
9. **Run quality gate** — Execute `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e` and fix any issues.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `isResourceTimingSupported` with both present and absent PerformanceObserver (mock/delete globalThis.PerformanceObserver)
Unit test `extractTimingEntry` with realistic PerformanceResourceTiming mock data, verifying all fields map correctly
Unit test `ResourceTimingCollector` graceful degradation: start()/stop()/getEntries() must not throw when API is unavailable, getEntries() returns empty array
Unit test rolling window: push more entries than window size, verify oldest are evicted
Unit test `computeMeanDuration`: empty array returns 0, single entry returns that value, filters out non-finite values
Unit test `normalizeLoadDuration`: clamps at 0 and 1, handles NaN→0, handles Infinity→1
E2E test: verify ResourceTimingCollector picks up at least one entry after a fetch in a real browser
E2E test: verify no console errors during resource timing collection
Run full quality gate: lint, typecheck, test, test:e2e
risks: PerformanceObserver.supportedEntryTypes may not include 'resource' in all target browsers — mitigated by feature detection with try/catch fallback
Cross-origin resource entries report zero duration per Timing-Allow-Origin spec — mitigated by filtering to same-origin entries and documenting this constraint in the ADR
Unit testing PerformanceObserver requires mocking browser globals in Node environment — mitigated by using vi.stubGlobal and careful mock setup following existing probeSampler test patterns
Rolling window size (50) may be too large or too small depending on page activity — mitigated by making it configurable via options, with 50 as a sensible default
transferSize may be 0 even for same-origin if served from cache — treat as expected behavior, do not depend on transferSize for primary metrics
